question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the startup probe does not verify the database's trigger state, so that the importer starts against a database where record-processing triggers are disabled, causing side effects (e.g., balance updates) to be skipped and incorrect aggregate data to be exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the startup probe does not verify the database's partition state for time-partitioned record tables, so that the importer starts against a database with missing partitions for the current time range, causing record inserts to fail with partition-not-found errors and those records to be missing from mirror node exports?
